title: Resume
text:
Download PDF Resume  View on LinkedIn
Note: I tailor my resume for each application - feel free to reach out if you’d like a version targeted to a specific role.
Data Scientist with a PhD in Psychology and 15+ years translating complex, real-world problems into high-impact data solutions. Expert at building data infrastructure, interactive tools, and automated reporting systems that drive strategic decisions. Proven ability to lead end-to-end projects across the full data lifecycle - from requirements gathering and pipeline development through deployment and stakeholder communication. Available for full-time and contract engagements.

title: Resume
text:
Impact Highlights

Built financial tracking system in 3 days to monitor $5.45B, replacing a manual 10-report compilation process
Designed and deployed a workforce simulation app in 1 week - proving need for 100 additional staff and compressing the timeline from 3 years to 40 weeks
Drove on-time acknowledgment rate from 24% → 51% in 3 weeks, reducing a 1,000-claim backlog via automated acknowledgment system
Integrated 25M+ data points from 14 disparate sources for a longitudinal study of 400 participants

title: Resume
text:

title: Resume
text:
Professional Experience
Lead Data Scientist, Consultant | ABS Group · Santa Fe, NM Mar 2024 – Mar 2026
Data analytics team supporting FEMA’s $5.45B Hermit’s Peak/Calf Canyon Claims Office (30,000+ claims)

Built interactive workforce simulation app in one week - modeling 5-stage claims process across 11 teams, proving need for 100 additional staff to meet 40-week deadline vs. initial 3-year estimate; directly supported executive staffing justification
Engineered automated financial tracking system replacing 10-report manual compilation process; enabled executive monitoring of $5.45B across three payment tiers with min/max scenario planning, informing critical program and resource allocation decisions
Conducted predictive modeling of future workforce performance based on historical individual and team data, informing workforce rebalancing and capacity planning
Designed and deployed daily operations report serving 300+ staff across 3 sites with role-specific views (executive, site, team, individual), iteratively evolving based on stakeholder feedback
Developed automated acknowledgment system reducing claim backlog by 1,000 claims and increasing on-time acknowledgment rate from 24% to 51% in 3 weeks
Established complete data infrastructure from scratch - database connectivity, data dictionary, automated daily pipeline, standardized R/Excel protocols - creating single source of truth for all analytics; documented protocols and mentored team members
Consulted with senior leadership on analytical priorities; handled diverse requests ranging from 1-hour ad-hoc queries to multi-week dashboard development


Data Science Consultant | UC Davis · Remote Jan 2020 – Jun 2024
Contemplative Coping During COVID-19 Study: Longitudinal multidisciplinary outcomes study examining health and wellbeing of ~400 U.S. participants during COVID-19

Designed and implemented data pipeline integrating 25M+ data points from 14 disparate sources (Qualtrics surveys, biological samples) for longitudinal study of 400 participants
Established reproducible R/Rmarkdown/Quarto protocols adopted by 15 researchers, ensuring transparent and auditable data pipelines across the full study lifecycle
Automated generation of individualized mental health outcome reports for 350+ participants using Quarto/R, delivering personalized insights at scale
Directed clustering and multivariate distance matrix regression analyses of mixed-methods data to identify predictors of participant outcomes and intervention preferences
Collaborated with stakeholders to iteratively develop interactive R Shiny outcomes dashboard, enabling researchers to explore study insights and communicate findings; contributed to 4 conference presentations


Chief Program Officer / Head of Education | Think Elephants International · Remote May 2010 – Jun 2024

Designed and analyzed program evaluations using 1,500+ pre/post-intervention surveys across participant and control groups; led survey design, data collection, and sampling methodology
Directed multilevel modeling, factor analysis, and nonparametric statistics in R/R Markdown to assess learning outcomes and identify instructional improvement opportunities
Coordinated education programs across NY, CA, and Thailand serving 600+ students at 18 sites; managed teams of 3–9 instructors and 20+ partner organizations
Supervised and mentored 18 junior researchers including 3 thesis students; disseminated findings through 5 conference presentations, 2 reports, and 1 publication


Freelance Data Consultant | Remote 2023 – 2024

Adelphi University (2024): Automated exploratory data analysis, principal component analysis (PCA), and inferential statistics workflows in R
UC San Diego StayWELL Project (2023): Developed interactive R Shiny data visualization tool to communicate wellbeing study findings to stakeholders; engaged in client-facing discussions to identify needs and provide strategic recommendations


Researcher · UC Davis, Center for Mind and Brain · Davis, CA 2013 – 2018

Contributed to $1.5M longitudinal research project assessing meditation intervention outcomes via cross-sectional study and randomized controlled trial (RCT) with wait-list control group; analyzed survey, behavioral, physiological, and biological data from 10+ experiments
Initiated and led development of novel network analytic method for quantifying qualitative data; published in peer-reviewed journal and presented at research symposium
Collaborated across multidisciplinary stakeholder teams, contributing to 3 publications and 8 conference presentations
Supervised and mentored 20+ junior team members in research methodology and statistical analysis


Earlier research experience at UC Davis MIND Institute and Emory University - see full PDF for complete history

title: Jen Pokorny, PhD
text:
I don’t just analyze data - I build the tools and infrastructure that turn it into decisions.
View My Work Download Resume

title: About
text:
I build data infrastructure, interactive tools, and automated reporting systems that help organizations make better decisions - faster. With 15+ years working across research, consulting, and industry, I’ve learned that the most valuable data work rarely starts with data. It starts with understanding what people actually need, which is often different from what they think they need.
My work spans the full data lifecycle: from integrating messy, real-world datasets and building pipelines from scratch, to deploying Shiny apps and dashboards used daily. Most recently, I spent two years embedded with FEMA’s Hermit’s Peak Claims Office - building complete data infrastructure for the operations analysis team for a $5.45B program, including a workforce simulation app that compressed an estimated 3-year staffing timeline to 40 weeks.

title: About
text:
I build data infrastructure, interactive tools, and automated reporting systems that help organizations make better decisions - faster. With 15+ years working across research, consulting, and industry, I’ve learned that the most valuable data work rarely starts with data. It starts with understanding what people actually need, which is often different from what they think they need.
My work spans the full data lifecycle: from integrating messy, real-world datasets and building pipelines from scratch, to deploying Shiny apps and dashboards used daily. Most recently, I spent two years embedded with FEMA’s Hermit’s Peak Claims Office - building complete data infrastructure for the operations analysis team for a $5.45B program, including a workforce simulation app that compressed an estimated 3-year staffing timeline to 40 weeks.

title: About
text:
How I Work
I thrive in environments where the infrastructure doesn’t exist yet - where the job is to figure out what’s needed, build it, and make sure people actually use it. I’m equally comfortable scoping a complex multi-month system and turning around a critical ad-hoc analysis in an afternoon.
I’m a strong advocate for open, reproducible data pipelines - work that doesn’t fall apart when someone else has to maintain it. I thoughtfully integrate generative AI tools throughout my workflow to accelerate delivery without sacrificing rigor, and I design solutions for the people who will actually use them rather than a hypothetical average user.
My background includes a PhD in Psychology, which shapes how I approach every engagement: I listen carefully, ask the questions behind the questions, and bring a particular curiosity to work that sits at the intersection of data and human wellbeing - health outcomes, workforce dynamics, and the factors that shape how people and organizations function.

title: About
text:
What I’m Looking For
I’ve worked across academic research, federal consulting, nonprofit program evaluation, and industry - on both long-term W2 engagements and independent contract projects. That range has made me fast to orient and contribute, regardless of environment or domain.
I’m currently open to full-time data scientist roles and contract engagements, with particular interest in health, workforce, and operations contexts where data can drive meaningful impact.

title: Portfolio
text:
A sample of interactive data tools I’ve built using R and Shiny. Each app was designed to make complex data accessible - whether for research teams, study participants, or anyone curious about a dataset.
Additional projects are in development, adapted from consulting work to demonstrate transferable capabilities.

title: Portfolio
text:
CCC Study Dashboard





CCC Study Dashboard - interactive outcomes dashboard built for a UC Davis longitudinal COVID-19 study




What it does: An interactive outcomes dashboard built for a UC Davis research team studying mindfulness-based coping during the COVID-19 pandemic. Allows researchers to explore participant stress exposures and wellbeing outcomes across multiple time points and filter by demographic and practice variables.
The challenge: Integrating 25M+ data points from 14 disparate sources into a single coherent interface that both researchers and non-technical stakeholders could navigate.
Built with: R · Shiny · ggplot2 · plotly · tidyverse · exploratory data analysis
Context: Academic research project, UC Davis

 View App  View Code

title: Portfolio
text:
StayWELL Study App





StayWELL Study App - interactive data visualization tool built for UC San Diego




What it does: An interactive data visualization tool built for the UC San Diego StayWELL Project, which assessed the wellbeing of older adults during the COVID-19 pandemic, enabling researchers to explore and share study findings with other investigators and stakeholders. Designed to present ellbeing data in a format accessible to both research and clinical audiences.
The challenge: Translating complex longitudinal wellbeing data into a navigable interface that serves multiple audiences - from principal investigators to clinicians unfamiliar with raw study output.
Built with: R · Shiny · ggplot2 · plotly · tidyverse
Context: Freelance contract, UC San Diego (2023)

 View App

title: Portfolio
text:
Name Explorer





Name Explorer - interactive tool for exploring U.S. baby name trends




What it does: An interactive explorer for U.S. baby name trends, letting users search by name, filter by starting letter, length, or contained string, and visualize popularity over time by year and gender. A fun tool that demonstrates clean UI design and responsive data exploration on a universally relatable dataset.
The challenge: Making a large, multi-dimensional dataset genuinely fun and fast to explore - with enough filter combinations to satisfy a curious user without overwhelming them.
Built with: R · Shiny · ggplot2 · plotly · tidyverse
Context: Personal project
